summary: Crisp soundboard capture of a stellar late‑1972 Wichita concert, highlighted by an epic Bird Song and a soaring The Other One.
review: The recording is a clean, well‑balanced Mike Lai soundboard transfer that reveals excellent instrument separation, though it runs a few cents sharp and the Jack Straw segment suffers a noticeable cut. Musically the show shines with standout moments – a radiant Bird Song, an exploratory jam on The Other One, gorgeous Brokedown Palace and punchy Truckin' → Other One, plus strong performances from Jerry, Phil and Keith. Overall the energy stays high throughout both sets, making this a must‑listen for fans of the 1972 era.

ai_rating.rationale: All reviews are 5‑star with consistent praise for both performance and sound quality; minor technical quirks (pitch, Jack Straw cut) keep the rating just below perfect.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, clear mix with great instrument separation. A few cents sharp in the Lai transfer and a noticeable Jack Straw cut, but overall very listenable.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Solid first set with tight playing; the second set builds into expansive, exploratory jams and seamless transitions, maintaining high energy from start to finish.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s guitar tone is praised as scorching on Me & My Uncle and driving throughout the jam-heavy portions.
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil’s vocals are highlighted as sounding like the studio version on Box of Rain and generally awesome throughout.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bob’s vocal contributions (e.g., Bird Song) are noted for shining on this show.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith is lauded for brilliant jazz‑piano work during The Other One and overall superb playing.
